entry.title: Machine Learning
entry.content:
I wanted to learn to program well in C++ – interesting things hapen in C++, and I’ve also wanted to build up a thorough understanding of the fundamentals of machine learning; so of course I thought I’d kill two birds with one stone and learn C++ while writing a machine learning library from scrach.

I’m not coming in completely cold: I used C++ for a simulation project on my engineering degree – but it was the work of a self-taught engineering student, and it shows. I’ve also studied the fundamentals of machine learning when I studied computer science, but need to blow off the cobwebs.

I’ve started off making some utilities, point classes (I’m working with numeric coordinates for the time being). I’ve move on to implementing simple things - first port of call was the perceptron of course. Next step was to make a simple multiclass version (really a tagger).

I’m currently working on implementing a basic SVM, and on addding some evaluation tools.
entry.summary: I wanted to learn to program well in C++ – interesting things hapen in C++, and I’ve also wanted to build up a thorough understanding of the fundamentals of machine learning; so of course I thought I’d kill two birds with one stone and learn C++ while writing a machine learning library from scrach.

entry.title: Memelord
entry.content: I wrote this to play around with OCR through Tesseract. It reads the text in memes and makes a folder of them seaerchable. It is currently a trivial bit of programming - I might try to add unsupervised detection of different meme formats at some point, and hook it up to a GUI.
entry.summary: I wrote this to play around with OCR through Tesseract. It reads the text in memes and makes a folder of them seaerchable. It is currently a trivial bit of programming - I might try to add unsupervised detection of different meme formats at some point, and hook it up to a GUI.

entry.title: Twitter Analytics Dashboard
entry.content:
I wrote this for my MSc dissertation. It is an electron app that provides some generic tools for gathering and analysing a large set of tweets. It contains the normal NLP text cleaning functions, a function to gather tweets through Twitter’s API, plotting, and some ML tools.

Update: the Twitter API used here was depricated.
entry.summary: I wrote this for my MSc dissertation. It is an electron app that provides some generic tools for gathering and analysing a large set of tweets. It contains the normal NLP text cleaning functions, a function to gather tweets through Twitter’s API, plotting, and some ML tools.
